$comment: Ch 3 — THE FADE, scenario 2 (slight backward drift). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Schema mirrors bb-1-baseline.json. Fade represented by increasing effective distance vs the standstill control (5.0 → 5.5 m). Lesson copy carries the body-momentum concept.

meta.title: STEPPED BACK.
meta.subtitle: CH 3 — THE FADE, SMALL DRIFT
meta.authorIntent: A half-meter backward drift from the standstill control. d=5.5, h_r=2.3, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=55°, v=7.96 m/s. Verification: t = 5.5 / (7.96·cos 55°) = 1.205 s; y(1.205) = 2.3 + 7.96·sin(55°)·1.205 − ½·9.8·1.205² = 3.043 m — matches h_h (err 0.007 m). Pedagogical puzzle: a user who naively reuses the standstill answer (55°, 7.63) falls short. The half-meter extra distance demands +0.33 m/s — exactly the chapter's insight: a fade adds distance you didn't think you'd added.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. Fading away costs reach every time.
subheadVariants.2: Still short. The body's drift needs to be paid for.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. The body's drift was never paid for.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Lift the arc.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle higher.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Overcorrected for the step-back.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long. Too much push to fight a small drift.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. The body went one way, the ball went nowhere.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=55°, v=7.96 m/s. Same arc shape as the standstill, +0.33 m/s of velocity — the visible cost of the fade.

hints.body: The fade adds distance. The same shot doesn't reach the same place anymore.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
d is bigger than the standstill. Velocity will be too.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Hold θ near 55°; solve for the higher v that the extra distance demands.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 7.96 m/s.

intro.headline: STEPPED BACK.
intro.subhead: A small backward step. The ball inherits the drift.

subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Iron held a fading ball. Generous.

miss.headline: DRIFTED SHORT

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: θ near 55°, push v near 8.0.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 55°, cut v by ~0.4.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 55°, push v near 8.0.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
